task_completion.description: Verify the Home root page is created and mentions 'TailwindTraders' and 'e-commerce'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A wiki Home page is visible in the screenshots containing the text 'TailwindTraders' and 'e-commerce'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'System Overview' page exists under an 'Architecture' section, describes microservices, and names at least three services.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A page titled 'System Overview' (nested under 'Architecture') is visible, with content discussing microservices and explicitly naming 3 or more services.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'API Design' page exists under 'Architecture', mentions REST, includes an endpoint example, and lists HTTP status codes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A page titled 'API Design' is visible, containing the word 'REST', an endpoint example like 'GET /api...', and HTTP status codes (e.g., 200, 404).

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Getting Started' page exists under a 'Development Guide' section, and includes 'Prerequisites', a 'git clone' command, and numbered steps.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A page titled 'Getting Started' is visible, showing a 'Prerequisites' section, a 'git clone' command, and a numbered list of instructions.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Coding Standards' page exists under 'Development Guide', and includes a code block and naming conventions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A page titled 'Coding Standards' is visible, containing at least one formatted code block and text discussing naming conventions.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Incident Response' page exists under a 'Runbooks' section, and defines severity levels and escalation steps.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A page titled 'Incident Response' is visible, containing text that defines severity levels (e.g., Sev1, Sev2) and outlines escalation steps.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with a wiki interface or web browser rather than just writing raw markdown files in a local text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser or dedicated wiki application interface with navigation menus, not just a code editor like VS Code.

integrity.description: Verify the agent generated actual documentation content rather than just copy-pasting the literal task description.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text on the wiki pages should be formatted as natural documentation expanding on the requirements, rather than a verbatim copy-paste of the prompt.

integrity.description: Verify the agent successfully created the pages rather than just writing a script that failed to execute.
integrity.visual_evidence: The rendered wiki pages or successful creation confirmations should be visible in the screenshots, not just terminal commands or unexecuted scripts.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The complete expected wiki hierarchy is 9 pages: Home (/), Architecture, Architecture/System Overview, Architecture/API Design, Development Guide, Development Guide/Getting Started, Development Guide/Coding Standards, Runbooks, and Runbooks/Incident Response. The Home page must contain 'TailwindTraders' and 'e-commerce'. The API Design page should include keywords like 'REST', 'GET', 'POST', '200', and '404'. The Incident Response page should include keywords like 'severity', 'Sev1', 'Sev2', and 'escalation'.
